class.name: AudioStreamMP3
class.brief_description: Stores audio data in MP3 format as an [AudioStream] resource.
class.description: [AudioStreamMP3] stores audio samples from loaded MP3 files. MP3 files can be loaded with [method load_from_file]. See [ResourceImporterMP3] for information on how MP3 files are imported as [AudioStream] resources. To play the stored audio, use an [AudioStreamPlayer] (for non-positional audio) or [AudioStreamPlayer2D]/[AudioStreamPlayer3D] (for positional audio). [b]Note:[/b] This class can optionally support legacy MP1 and MP2 formats, provided that the engine is compiled with the [code]minimp3_extra_formats=yes[/code] SCons option. These extra formats are not enabled by default. See [url=https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine_details/development/compiling/index.html]Building from source[/url] to learn how to compile the engine. [b]Note:[/b] MP3 files always have a short period (measured in milliseconds) of silence at the beginning. The length of this header is variable. Because of this silence, this format is not perfect for beat-related features. If perfect synchronization is desired, consider using [AudioStreamOggVorbis]. If you convert an MP3 file to Ogg Vorbis (it is preferred to start [i]from[/i] a lossless format), the silent header must be trimmed first.

method.name: load_from_buffer
method.description: Creates a new [AudioStreamMP3] instance from the given buffer. The buffer must contain MP3 data.

method.name: load_from_file
method.description: Creates a new [AudioStreamMP3] instance from the given file path. The file must be in MP3 format. [b]Note:[/b] This method should only be used to load [i]external[/i] files at runtime. To load files that were previously imported into Godot as resources, use a method like [method @GDScript.load].

members.member: The number of beats within a single bar of the audio.

The length of the audio, in beats. The actual duration of the audio file might be longer than what is indicated by this property. It defines the end of the audio for looping, [AudioStreamPlaylist], and [AudioStreamInteractive].
The tempo of the audio, measured in beats per minute (BPM).
Contains the audio data in bytes.

If [code]true[/code], the stream will play again from the specified [member loop_offset] once it reaches the end of the stream, or once it reaches the end of the last beat according to the number specified in [member beat_count]. Useful for ambient sounds and background music.

Playback position in seconds at which the stream starts after being looped. This value should range from [code]0.0[/code] to the length of the stream in seconds, or to the end of the beat set in [member beat_count] in seconds, if the stream loops at a set beat.
